articles.title: Congress buys time on surveillance powers, setting up another fight at month’s end
articles.summary: Lawmakers rushed through a short-term renewal of a controversial intelligence tool after a late-night House revolt derailed President Donald Trump’s push for a longer extension.

articles.title: IMF says tariffs have left U.S. inflation flat, but the next phase may be trickier
articles.summary: A new IMF review says tariff-driven goods inflation has offset softer services prices, keeping inflation stuck in place as growth holds up — for now.

articles.title: NASA’s Lucy Finds a Surprising Asteroid with a Strange Double-Lobed Past
articles.summary: New data from the Lucy mission’s flyby of asteroid Donaldjohanson suggest the object may be a contact binary with a more complex formation history than scientists expected.

articles.title: Cannes 2026 lineup tilts toward auteurs as Hollywood keeps its distance
articles.summary: The 79th Cannes Film Festival has unveiled a competition packed with international auteurs, while major U.S. studios again hold back, underscoring how the world’s most influential film festival is shaping this year’s awards and sales conversation.

articles.title: OpenAI’s new security scare, compute push, and child-safety agenda show the AI race’s new front line
articles.summary: A week of rapid moves at OpenAI — from a macOS security response to a $38 billion AWS pact and a child-safety blueprint — underscores how AI competition is shifting toward trust, infrastructure and policy.

articles.title: Meta unveils Muse Spark as its newest AI model, signaling a faster push into personal superintelligence
articles.summary: Meta says its new Muse Spark model will power a smarter Meta AI across apps and glasses, while opening a private API preview to select partners.

articles.title: Why 2016 nostalgia is still defining 2026 culture
articles.summary: A wave of throwback posts, old filters and decade-old pop references is showing how online nostalgia has become a mainstream mood — and a market.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz gamble leaves ceasefire hanging over wider Middle East war
articles.summary: Iran briefly reopened the Strait of Hormuz, then reversed course after the U.S. kept its blockade in place, underscoring how fragile the new Lebanon truce and broader diplomacy remain.

articles.title: Coachella’s second weekend is arriving with a bigger pop-culture ripple than the first
articles.summary: Justin Bieber’s viral reset, Olivia Rodrigo’s fresh single and a stacked festival bill have turned Coachella 2026 into a weeklong pop-culture checkpoint.

articles.title: Sudan war enters a fourth year as aid officials warn of a global failure
articles.summary: Three years after fighting began, the world’s largest displacement crisis is still getting worse, with hunger deepening, hospitals collapsing and diplomacy failing to catch up.
